commands.review_summary:
The PR changes backup config saving to use a same-directory temporary file with sync, chmod, rename, and cleanup, plus tests for 0600 mode and failed final rename behavior.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence torn-write reproduction was established. Source inspection does confirm the vulnerable current-main path: backup init calls SaveConfig, and SaveConfig rewrites backup.json directly with os.WriteFile.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the branch is focused, but merge should stay human-gated until real behavior proof is added and maintainers decide the residual durability scope.

target.title: Save backup config atomically
target.body:
Closes #24.

Summary:
- Writes backup.json through a same-directory temporary file, syncs it, and renames it into place instead of truncating the existing config directly.
- Preserves 0600 permissions and cleans up temporary files on failure.

Behavior proof:
- A focused test forces the final rename to fail after staging the new config. LoadConfig still reads the original repo and age recipient, and no temporary config file remains.
- The config round-trip test verifies saved backup.json remains mode 0600.
target.author: turbotheturtle
